declarations.description:
Form-associated text input with a suggestion dropdown. Works as a
drop-in replacement for a plain `<input name="address">`: consumers keep
reading `new FormData(form).get("address")` and calling `form.reset()`
unchanged.

Supports two suggestion sources:
- **API** (default): fetches from `endpoint`, a Mapbox Geocoding v6-shaped
  forward-geocode URL, debounced as the user types.
- **Local array**: set `suggestions` to a fixed `AddressSuggestion[]` and
  the component filters it client-side instead of making any network
  request. Useful for small/fixed address lists, offline use, or tests.
  Takes priority over `endpoint` whenever it's non-null.
declarations.name: AddressAutocomplete

members.name: value
members.description: Current input value; also the form-associated value submitted with the form.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: Placeholder text shown when the input is empty.

members.name: required
members.description: Marks the input as required for native form validation.

members.name: types
members.description: Mapbox `types` param, e.g. "address" or "address,poi".

members.name: endpoint
members.description: Geocoding endpoint URL. Defaults to the Mapbox Geocoding v6 forward URL.

members.name: accessToken
members.description: Mapbox access token. Required for requests to succeed.

members.name: bbox
members.description: Optional bounding box bias, comma-separated `west,south,east,north`.

members.name: proximity
members.description: Optional proximity bias, comma-separated `lng,lat`.

members.name: debounce
members.description: Debounce delay (ms) before firing a geocode request after typing.

members.name: minLength
members.description: Minimum query length before suggestions are fetched.

members.name: suggestions
members.description:
Fixed candidate list to filter locally instead of fetching from
`endpoint`. When set (non-null), no network request is ever made —
this takes priority over the API mode.

members.name: selectedSuggestion
members.description: Last-picked suggestion, or null once the input diverges from it.

members.name: formResetCallback
members.description: Resets to empty on `form.reset()`, per the form-associated custom element contract.

members.name: #filterLocalSuggestions
members.description: Filters the locally-supplied `suggestions` list for `query`, case-insensitively.

members.name: #fetchSuggestions
members.description: Fetches suggestions for `query`, cancelling any request still in flight.

members.name: #onSuggestionMousedown
members.description: Selects on mousedown (not click) so it wins over the input's blur, which closes the list.

events.name: address-select
events.description: A suggestion was picked; detail: AddressSuggestion.

attributes.description: Marks the input as required for native form validation.

declarations.description:
Fullscreen confetti animation overlay, rendered on a fixed-position canvas.
Starts automatically on first render and stops after `duration` ms.
declarations.name: AnimateConfetti

members.name: duration
members.description: How long the confetti streams before stopping, in ms.

members.name: _handleResize
members.description: Resize canvas to browser size.

members.name: stopConfetti
members.description: Stop confetti and clean up after reasonable time.

members.name: _cleanup
members.description: Removes any associated particle data from internal storage to regain memory.

attributes.name: duration
attributes.description: How long the confetti streams before stopping, in ms.

declarations.description:
Generic form-associated text input with a suggestion dropdown, for any
"type to filter a list of `{key, value}` options" use case. Works as a
drop-in replacement for a plain `<input>` inside a `<form>`: set `name` on
the element itself and consumers keep reading
`new FormData(form).get(name)` and calling `form.reset()` unchanged — the
submitted value is the picked option's `value`, while `key` is available
via the `option-select` event and the `selectedOption` getter for cases
that need the underlying id rather than the display text.

Supports two suggestion sources:
- **API**: set `endpoint` to a URL that, given a `?<query-param>=<text>`
  query string, responds with a JSON array of `{key, value}` objects.
  Requests are debounced as the user types.
- **Local array**: set `options` to a fixed `AutocompleteOption[]` and the
  component filters it client-side instead of making any network
  request. Useful for small/fixed lists, offline use, or tests. Takes
  priority over `endpoint` whenever it's non-null.
declarations.name: AutocompleteInput

members.name: value
members.description: Current input value; also the form-associated value submitted with the form.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: Placeholder text shown when the input is empty.

members.name: required
members.description: Marks the input as required for native form validation.

members.description: API endpoint queried in API mode. Ignored when `options` is set.

members.name: queryParam
members.description: Query string parameter name the current input text is sent under.

members.name: debounce
members.description: Debounce delay (ms) before firing a request/filter after typing.

members.description: Minimum query length before suggestions are shown.

this takes priority over the API mode.

members.description: Resets to empty on `form.reset()`, per the form-associated custom element contract.

members.description: Filters the locally-supplied `options` list for `query`, case-insensitively.

members.name: #fetchOptions
members.description: Fetches options for `query` from `endpoint`, cancelling any request still in flight.

members.name: #onSuggestionMousedown
members.description: Selects on mousedown (not click) so it wins over the input's blur, which closes the list.

events.description: An option was picked; detail: AutocompleteOption.

declarations.description:
Reusable confirmation dialog: overlay + centered card with a slotted body,
an optional error line, and Cancel/Confirm actions. Instant `display:none`
→ `display:flex` toggle (no transitions). Fires `confirm`/`cancel`
(bubbling, composed) instead of owning any deletion logic itself —
callers stay in charge of the request.
declarations.name: ConfirmDialog

members.name: open
members.description: Whether the dialog is visible.

members.name: confirmLabel
members.description: Label for the confirm button.

members.name: cancelLabel
members.description: Label for the cancel button.

members.name: danger
members.description: Danger (red) vs. primary (indigo) styling for the confirm button.

members.name: busy
members.description: Shows a spinner and disables both buttons while a request is in flight.

members.name: error
members.description: Inline error line shown below the body, or null for none.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Whether the dialog is visible.

attributes.name: confirm-label
attributes.description: Label for the confirm button.

attributes.name: cancel-label
attributes.description: Label for the cancel button.

attributes.description: Danger (red) vs. primary (indigo) styling for the confirm button.

attributes.description: Shows a spinner and disables both buttons while a request is in flight.

attributes.name: error
attributes.description: Inline error line shown below the body, or null for none.

declarations.description:
Small icon button that copies `value` to the clipboard and shows a toast
on success/failure (if a `<toast-notification>` element is present), and
always dispatches a `copy-success`/`copy-error` CustomEvent so consumers
without a toast element can react. Defaults to the current page URL if
`value` is unset.
declarations.name: CopyLinkButton

members.name: value
members.description: Text to copy. Defaults to `window.location.href` at click time.

members.name: label
members.description: Accessible label / tooltip text.

events.name: copy-success
events.description: The value was copied to the clipboard.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Text to copy. Defaults to `window.location.href` at click time.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Accessible label / tooltip text.

declarations.description:
Inline distance display. Renders miles/feet or km/m, switching units at
sensible thresholds (< 0.25 mi → ft; < 0.5 km → m).

Supply exactly one of `miles` or `km`; the other stays null.
km support is present but reserved for future use.
declarations.name: DistanceValue

members.name: miles
members.description: Distance in miles (imperial). Switches to feet below 0.25 mi.

members.name: km
members.description: Distance in kilometers (metric, future). Switches to meters below 0.5 km.

attributes.name: miles
attributes.description: Distance in miles (imperial). Switches to feet below 0.25 mi.

attributes.name: km
attributes.description: Distance in kilometers (metric, future). Switches to meters below 0.5 km.

declarations.description:
Renders a KDE distribution curve for a named metric with one or more value
markers. The SVG viewBox is kept in sync with the element's pixel width via
ResizeObserver so that font sizes and stroke widths are always in real pixels
regardless of container width.

Pass `fontSize` to control all text size (default 11). Pass a single
`{label:'', value}` for a single-value display or multiple
`{label:'A'|'B'|...}` entries to compare several values.
declarations.name: DistributionChart

members.name: metric
members.description: Metric name, fetched from `/api/distribution/<metric>` on change.

members.name: values
members.description: One or more values to mark on the distribution curve.

members.name: markerColors
members.description: Colors assigned to markers in order. Defaults to indigo/amber/teal/rose 600s.

members.name: fontSize
members.description: Target font size in CSS pixels (default 11). Always renders at this size regardless of container width.

members.name: _width
members.description: Measured element width in CSS pixels. 0 until ResizeObserver fires.

members.name: _hoverX
members.description: Inner x coordinate (px) of the current hover position, or null when not hovering.

attributes.name: metric
attributes.description: Metric name, fetched from `/api/distribution/<metric>` on change.

attributes.name: font-size
attributes.description: Target font size in CSS pixels (default 11). Always renders at this size regardless of container width.

declarations.description:
A plain circular map marker: a light-to-dark gradient fill with a white
outer ring, no point/tail (unlike `<map-pin>`) — for markers that don't
need to visually "point" at their exact coordinate. Purely a visual
primitive — it has no `mapbox-gl` (or any mapping library) dependency;
the consumer positions it, e.g. via `new mapboxgl.Marker({ element: el })`.
declarations.name: MapCircle

slots.description: Optional badge content shown centered on the circle — a rank number, an emoji, a small icon.

members.name: color
members.description: Fill color; the gradient's light (top) and dark (bottom) stops are derived from this.

members.name: size
members.description: Diameter, in CSS pixels.

members.name: ringWidth
members.description: White outer ring thickness, in the same viewBox units as `size` (scales with it).

members.name: highlighted
members.description: Scales and glows the circle — a generic emphasis state (e.g. hover, selection).

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: Fill color; the gradient's light (top) and dark (bottom) stops are derived from this.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: Diameter, in CSS pixels.

attributes.name: ring-width
attributes.description: White outer ring thickness, in the same viewBox units as `size` (scales with it).

attributes.name: highlighted
attributes.description: Scales and glows the circle — a generic emphasis state (e.g. hover, selection).

declarations.description:
A circular "Apple Maps"-style map pin: a light-to-dark gradient fill with
a slight point at the bottom. Purely a visual primitive — it has no
`mapbox-gl` (or any mapping library) dependency; the consumer positions
it, e.g. via `new mapboxgl.Marker({ element: pinEl })`.
declarations.name: MapPin

slots.description: Badge content shown centered on the pin's circular head — a rank number, an emoji, a small icon.

members.name: color
members.description: Fill color; the gradient's light (top) and dark (bottom) stops are derived from this.

members.name: size
members.description: Diameter of the circular head, in CSS pixels.

members.name: highlighted
members.description: Scales and glows the pin — a generic emphasis state (e.g. hover, selection).

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: Fill color; the gradient's light (top) and dark (bottom) stops are derived from this.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: Diameter of the circular head, in CSS pixels.

attributes.name: highlighted
attributes.description: Scales and glows the pin — a generic emphasis state (e.g. hover, selection).

declarations.description:
A small plain-colored map marker for dense point layers (transit stops,
amenities, hazard points, etc.): a light-to-dark gradient fill with a thin
white ring, no badge/content slot — every instance on a given layer shares
the same look, so there's nothing to render per-feature (unlike
`<map-pin>`/`<map-circle>`, which carry per-marker slotted content).
Purely a visual primitive — it has no `mapbox-gl` (or any mapping library)
dependency; typically rasterized once per color and used as a Mapbox
`icon-image` on a `symbol` layer rather than mounted as individual DOM
markers, so a whole layer's worth of points shares one icon image.
declarations.name: MapPoint

members.name: color
members.description: Fill color; the gradient's light (top) and dark (bottom) stops are derived from this.

members.name: size
members.description: Diameter, in CSS pixels.

members.name: ringWidth
members.description: White outer ring thickness, in the same viewBox units as `size` (scales with it).

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: Fill color; the gradient's light (top) and dark (bottom) stops are derived from this.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: Diameter, in CSS pixels.

attributes.name: ring-width
attributes.description: White outer ring thickness, in the same viewBox units as `size` (scales with it).

members.name: _handleSlotChange
members.description: Refreshes item state after the default slot changes.

members.name: _handleMouseEnter

members.name: _handleMouseLeave
members.description: Resumes autoplay with a fresh delay after hover ends.

members.name: _handleFocusIn
members.description: Temporarily pauses autoplay while focus is inside the gallery.

members.name: _handleFocusOut
members.description: Resumes autoplay with a fresh delay after focus leaves the gallery.

members.name: _handleVisibilityChange

members.name: _handleKeyDown

members.name: _handlePointerDown

members.name: _handlePointerUp
members.description: Converts a sufficiently horizontal pointer gesture into navigation.

members.name: _handlePointerCancel
members.description: Cancels an incomplete pointer gesture.

members.name: _renderPicture
members.description: Renders one responsive image layer for the crossfade stack.

members.name: _renderFooter
members.description: Renders enabled counter, indicator, and autoplay controls.

declarations.description:
D3-powered SVG line chart for property price history.

Uses scaleTime (X) + scaleLinear (Y) from d3-scale and line/area path
generators from d3-shape. Adapts to container width via ResizeObserver.
declarations.name: PriceHistoryChart

members.description: Array of price points (points with null price/date are skipped).

members.name: yLabels
members.description: "auto" (default) | "always" | "never"

members.name: maxXLabels
members.description: Max X-axis date ticks (default 3).

attributes.name: history
attributes.description: Array of price points (points with null price/date are skipped).

attributes.name: y-labels
attributes.description: "auto" (default) | "always" | "never"

attributes.name: max-x-labels
attributes.description: Max X-axis date ticks (default 3).

declarations.description:
Single-select group of full-width cards, each with a label and optional
description — for a handful of meaningfully different choices where the
description matters. For many short, same-shaped options (a color swatch,
a basemap style), use `radio-pills` instead. Wraps native radio inputs for
keyboard/a11y and fires `change` rather than relying on form submission.
declarations.name: RadioCards

members.name: options
members.description: Options to render, one card each.

members.name: value
members.description: Currently selected value.

events.name: change
events.description: A card was selected; detail: { value }.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Currently selected value.

declarations.description:
Single-select group of compact pill-shaped options — for many short,
same-shaped choices (a basemap style, a unit toggle). For a handful of
choices where a description matters, use `radio-cards` instead. Wraps
native radio inputs for keyboard/a11y and fires `change` rather than
relying on form submission.
declarations.name: RadioPills

members.name: options
members.description: Options to render, one pill each.

members.name: value
members.description: Currently selected value.

events.name: change
events.description: A pill was selected; detail: { value }.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Currently selected value.

declarations.description:
Inline relative-time display (e.g. "3 hours ago"). Accepts either a
standard ISO 8601 string or a SQLite `datetime('now')` string
("YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS", UTC, no zone marker) via `datetime`. Shows the
full date/time in the viewer's local timezone as a hover tooltip, and
re-renders on an interval so the text stays current while visible.
declarations.name: RelativeTime

members.name: datetime
members.description: Timestamp to render, relative to now.

attributes.name: datetime
attributes.description: Timestamp to render, relative to now.

declarations.description:
Generic sliding panel shell. Handles positioning, open/close animation,
header chrome, and a close button. Body content is provided via the
default slot; the consumer controls its own padding and overflow.

Desktop: 300 px fixed right-side panel that slides from the right.
Mobile (≤768px): bottom-sheet drawer (60vh) — reserved for future use.
declarations.name: SlidePanel

members.name: open
members.description: Whether the panel is currently visible.

members.name: heading
members.description: Title text shown in the panel header (overridable via slot="title").

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Whether the panel is currently visible.

attributes.name: heading
attributes.description: Title text shown in the panel header (overridable via slot="title").

declarations.description:
A compact labeled meter for a single percentage reading — e.g. CPU or
memory usage in a dashboard header. `percent` may be `null` when no
reading is available yet (e.g. the first tick of a polling metric); the
bar then renders empty and the value shows an em dash instead of "0%".
declarations.name: StatMeter

members.name: label
members.description: Short label shown before the bar, e.g. "CPU" or "MEM".

members.name: percent
members.description: Percentage 0-100. `null` renders an empty bar and a "—" value instead of "0%".

members.name: color
members.description: Fill color override; falls back to the `--ui-success` token.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Short label shown before the bar, e.g. "CPU" or "MEM".

attributes.name: percent
attributes.description: Percentage 0-100. `null` renders an empty bar and a "—" value instead of "0%".

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: Fill color override; falls back to the `--ui-success` token.

declarations.description:
Fixed-position stack of dismissible notifications, anchored top-right
(top-full-width on mobile). Not wired to any app state yet — callers add
toasts imperatively via the `show()` method on a live element reference,
e.g. `document.querySelector('toast-notification')?.show('Offline', { variant: 'error' })`,
or via the `notifySuccess`/`notifyError`/`notifyInfo` module-level helpers
exported from this file. Each toast auto-dismisses after `duration` ms and
can also be dismissed via its ✕ button. Appears/disappears instantly — no
slide/fade transitions.
declarations.name: ToastNotification

members.name: show
members.description: Queues a toast for display.

members.name: dismiss
members.description: Removes a toast immediately, cancelling its pending auto-dismiss timer if any.

declarations.name: notifyError
declarations.description: Shows an error toast. Use for connection/network failures, not validation errors.

declarations.name: notifySuccess
declarations.description: Shows a success toast.

declarations.name: notifyInfo
declarations.description: Shows an info toast.

declarations.name: tokenValues
declarations.default: { // Colors "--ui-primary": "#4f46e5", // indigo-600 "--ui-primary-hover": "#4338ca", // indigo-700 "--ui-danger": "#dc2626", // red-600 "--ui-danger-hover": "#b91c1c", // red-700 "--ui-success": "#16a34a", // green-600 "--ui-text": "#0f172a", // slate-900 "--ui-text-muted": "#64748b", // slate-500 "--ui-border": "#e2e8f0", // slate-200 "--ui-surface": "#ffffff", // white "--ui-surface-muted": "#f8fafc", // slate-50 "--ui-overlay": "rgb(15 23 42 / 0.45)", // slate-900 / 45% // Shape / depth "--ui-radius": "0.5rem", // rounded-lg "--ui-radius-sm": "0.25rem", // rounded "--ui-shadow": "0 4px 6px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1), 0 2px 4px -2px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1)", // shadow-md "--ui-shadow-lg": "0 20px 25px -5px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1), 0 8px 10px -6px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1)", // shadow-xl "--ui-focus-ring": "0 0 0 3px rgb(79 70 229 / 0.35)", // Type "--ui-font": 'ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif, "Apple Color Emoji", "Segoe UI Emoji", "Segoe UI Symbol", "Noto Color Emoji"', "--ui-font-size": "0.875rem", "--ui-font-size-sm": "0.75rem", }
declarations.description:
Design token values, sourced from Tailwind v4's default theme palette.
Each entry maps a `--ui-*` custom property name to its default value.
These are the same values baked in as `var(--ui-*, <fallback>)` fallbacks
throughout component styles, so components render correctly with zero
external CSS. Consumers may override any of these by setting the custom
property on `:root` or an ancestor element (see `tokens.css`).

declarations.name: tokens
declarations.default: css` :host { --ui-primary: var(--ui-primary, #4f46e5); --ui-primary-hover: var(--ui-primary-hover, #4338ca); --ui-danger: var(--ui-danger, #dc2626); --ui-danger-hover: var(--ui-danger-hover, #b91c1c); --ui-success: var(--ui-success, #16a34a); --ui-text: var(--ui-text, #0f172a); --ui-text-muted: var(--ui-text-muted, #64748b); --ui-border: var(--ui-border, #e2e8f0); --ui-surface: var(--ui-surface, #ffffff); --ui-surface-muted: var(--ui-surface-muted, #f8fafc); --ui-overlay: var(--ui-overlay, rgb(15 23 42 / 0.45)); --ui-radius: var(--ui-radius, 0.5rem); --ui-radius-sm: var(--ui-radius-sm, 0.25rem); --ui-shadow: var( --ui-shadow, 0 4px 6px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1), 0 2px 4px -2px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1) ); --ui-shadow-lg: var( --ui-shadow-lg, 0 20px 25px -5px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1), 0 8px 10px -6px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1) ); --ui-focus-ring: var(--ui-focus-ring, 0 0 0 3px rgb(79 70 229 / 0.35)); --ui-font: var( --ui-font, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif, "Apple Color Emoji", "Segoe UI Emoji", "Segoe UI Symbol", "Noto Color Emoji" ); --ui-font-size: var(--ui-font-size, 0.875rem); --ui-font-size-sm: var(--ui-font-size-sm, 0.75rem); } `
declarations.description:
A shared `:host` stylesheet that establishes the token custom properties
with their fallback values. Import and include this in every component's
`static styles` array alongside its component-specific `css` block.

Button (or link styled as one) with an optional leading icon, in three
visual weights. Set `href` to render an `<a>` instead of a `<button>` —
same styling either way — for cross-page navigation that should look like
an action button; a disabled/busy link stays a real `<a>` with
`aria-disabled` + `pointer-events: none` rather than losing its href.
Put the icon in the `icon` slot and the label in the default slot.

Form-associated (`type="submit"`/`"reset"`): the actual `<button>` lives in
this element's shadow root, which native HTML form association does not
cross into from an ancestor light-DOM `<form>`. `type="submit"`/`"reset"`
is instead wired through `ElementInternals.form` — the same mechanism
`address-autocomplete` uses to associate with an ancestor form.
declarations.name: UiButton

slots.description: Optional leading icon (e.g. an inline SVG).
slots.name: icon

members.name: variant
members.description: Visual weight.

members.name: href
members.description: Renders an `<a href="...">` instead of a `<button>` when set.

members.name: type
members.description: Native button `type`. Ignored when `href` is set.

members.name: disabled
members.description: Disables the control and dims it.

members.name: busy
members.description: Shows a spinner in place of the icon slot and disables the control.

members.name: _onClick
members.description: Drives submit/reset on the ancestor form via ElementInternals, since a shadow-DOM button can't do it natively.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description: Visual weight.

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: Renders an `<a href="...">` instead of a `<button>` when set.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: Native button `type`. Ignored when `href` is set.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the control and dims it.

attributes.name: busy
attributes.description: Shows a spinner in place of the icon slot and disables the control.

declarations.description:
Circular avatar. Shows `src` when it loads successfully; falls back to the
first letter of `name` (uppercased) if `src` is unset or fails to load
(e.g. an expired OAuth profile-photo URL); falls back further to a generic
person icon if `name` is also unset. A broken image never leaves a blank
circle.
declarations.name: UserAvatar

members.name: src
members.description: Image URL to show. Falls back to initials/icon if unset or it fails to load.

members.name: name
members.description: Source string for the fallback initial (e.g. a display name or email) — first character, uppercased.

members.name: size
members.description: Diameter in CSS pixels.

attributes.name: src
attributes.description: Image URL to show. Falls back to initials/icon if unset or it fails to load.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Source string for the fallback initial (e.g. a display name or email) — first character, uppercased.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: Diameter in CSS pixels.

declarations.description:
Sorted horizontal bar chart of labeled weights (normalized fractions
summing to ~1). Bars sort descending — the order IS the priority ranking.
Widths scale relative to the largest weight (which fills its track); the
percent labels carry the absolute values. Rows are keyed by item id
(repeat directive) so a re-render with new weights moves the existing
rows; bar widths animate via CSS, re-sorting is instant.
declarations.name: WeightBarChart

members.name: items
members.description: Items to render as weighted rows, sorted descending by value.

declarations.name: mixHex
declarations.description:
Linearly interpolates `base` toward `target` by `weightPercent`% (0-100)
in RGB space, returning a 6-digit hex string. Used to derive gradient
light/dark stops from a single base color — SVG presentation attributes
(`stop-color`) can't consume `color-mix()`/CSS custom properties, so this
computes plain hex instead (see CLAUDE.md's SVG token rule).

declarations.name: formatMiles
declarations.description:
Formats a distance in miles, switching to feet below 0.25 mi.
At ≥10 mi precision drops to whole miles.

declarations.name: formatKm
declarations.description:
Formats a distance in kilometers, switching to meters below 0.5 km.
At ≥10 km precision drops to whole kilometers. (Future use.)

declarations.name: parseTimestamp
declarations.description:
Parses a timestamp from either SQLite's `datetime('now')` (a space-separated
UTC string with no zone marker, e.g. "2026-07-15 10:23:00") or a standard
ISO 8601 string. JS's `Date` parser treats the space-separated form as
local time, so it's coerced to `T...Z` first to anchor it to UTC.

declarations.name: formatRelativeTime
declarations.description: Formats `date` relative to `now` (e.g. "3 hours ago", "in 2 days").
